rulings.Case Title: LUCIANO COSLOVICH et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 4, 2008 LINE 17. DEFENDANT HATHAWAY DINWIDDIE CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED BURDEN OF PROOF. THE OBJECTIONS TO SCHERSCHELL DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED AND WITHOUT THE DECLARATION, THE BURDEN OF PROOF WOULD STILL SHIFT. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT CREATED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. REQUEST FOR CONTINUANCE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS DELAYED SEEKING THE EVIDENCE AND THE COUNTY'S LACK OF HISTORICAL RECORDS DEMONSTRATES THAT PLAINTIFF IS NOT LIKELY TO OBTAIN THE EVIDENCE IT SEEKS. THE LACK OF EVIDENCE FINDS FURTHER SUPPORT IN THE DECLARATION OF MARK SCHERSCHEL. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: NEAL TREMBATH VS. JACOB MATHIS MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 4, 2008 LINE 3. DEFENDANT JACOB MATHIS'S Motion To Dismiss Action (Request For Judicial Notice) IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 8, 2008. THE COURT REQUIRES THAT THE OPPOSING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE THE COURT A SET OF COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS. WHEN SUBMITTING THE DUPLICATE COPIES, PLEASE PROVIDE A COVER LETTER TO THE COURT REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE SO THAT THE COURTESY COPIES WON'T BE MISPLACED WHEN RECEIVED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: DICKSON ADETUYI VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 4, 2008 LINE 7. PLAINTIFF DICKSON ADETUYI'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE LIMITED TO F.E.H.A. CAUSES OF ACTION, THE CLAIMS ARE NOT SUBJECT TO THE WORKERS' COMPENSATION EXCLUSIVE REMEDY DEFENSE. GANTT V SENTRY INS. CO. (1992) 1 CAL 4TH 1083, 1101; ACCARDI V SUPERIOR COURT (1993) 17 CAL APP 4TH 341, 347. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: BRENDAN FITZPATRICK VS. SAN FRANCISCO-BAY AREA COUNCIL., BOY SCOUTS OF et al**CASE TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY**
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Transferring Venue To San Mateo County Superior Court And Request For Award Of Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 4, 2008 LINE 10. DEFENDANT DOUG KAPOVICH'S Motion For Order Transferring Venue To San Mateo County Superior Court (And Request For Award Of Attorney Fees) IS GRANTED. NO DEFENDANT HAS A RESIDENCE IN SAN FRANCISCO. ALTHOUGH THE BOY SCOUTS OF AMERICA MAY BE SUED IN THIS COUNTY AS A FOREIGN CORPORATION THAT DID NOT MAINTAIN A PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS, THAT DOES NOT MAKE IT A RESIDENT. A CHANGE OF VENUE TO SAN MATEO COUNTY IS WARRANTED UNDER CARRUTH V SUPERIOR COURT (1978) 80 CAL APP 3D 215, 220. THE REQUEST FOR FEES IS DENIED. IT IS PREMATURE TO CONSIDER THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: SCHOEPP PROPERTIES VS. PONCHIANO CRUZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
